note: The all-satisfied control, and the reason a degenerate judge cannot win here. Three of the eight cases expect PASS and five expect BLOCK, so a judge that blocks everything scores 5 verdicts out of 8 and a judge that passes everything scores 3. Neither strategy beats reading the code, which is the only property that makes the other seven cases interpretable.

labels.text: a size of zero or less raises rather than looping forever
labels.why: the guard raises ValueError before range() is reached, and the message names the offending size. Without it range(0, n, 0) raises a bare ValueError from the interpreter and a negative size silently returns an empty list.

labels.text: every piece except possibly the last holds exactly size items
labels.why: the stride and the slice width are both size, so every slice is full except the final one, which holds len(items) % size when that is non-zero.
